# Analysis

## Ground Truth (GT) Simplified

Let me first simplify the GT numerical expression:

$$GT = -0.459914680311301 \cdot v \cdot (1 - v^2) - 1.87329057400732 \cdot v - 2.49934616662645 \cdot x \cdot (0.0579034145056311 \cdot t + 1) - 2.49934616662645 \cdot x$$

Expanding:
$$GT = -0.459914680311301 \cdot v + 0.459914680311301 \cdot v^3 - 1.87329057400732 \cdot v - 2.49934616662645 \cdot x \cdot 0.0579034145056311 \cdot t - 2.49934616662645 \cdot x - 2.49934616662645 \cdot x$$

$$GT = (-0.459914680311301 - 1.87329057400732) \cdot v + 0.459914680311301 \cdot v^3 - 0.144727685519498 \cdot t \cdot x - 4.99869233325290 \cdot x$$

$$GT = -2.33320525431862 \cdot v + 0.459914680311301 \cdot v^3 - 0.144727685519498 \cdot t \cdot x - 4.99869233325290 \cdot x$$

So the GT is a linear combination of: **v, vÂ³, tÂ·x, x** (with no constant term).

## Checking Candidates

The GT has the form: $A \cdot x + B \cdot v + C \cdot v^3 + D \cdot t \cdot x$ (no constant, no other terms).
